Best Software · Painting Contractors

Best Software for Painting Contractors (2026)

From quick residential repaint estimates to large commercial painting contracts — the right software saves hours every week.

Painting contractors have high job volume, thin margins, and crews that need to be precisely scheduled to avoid downtime. The best software for painters handles rapid quoting, crew dispatch, and job documentation — without requiring you to learn a platform built for homebuilders.

Quick comparison

SoftwareBest forStarting priceFree trial
Houzz ProResidential painters with design-conscious clients$85/mo30-day trial
JobberHigh-volume residential painting services$49/mo14-day trial
BuildertrendPainters integrated with GC/remodel teams$499/moNo (demo)
JobTreadMid-size painting companies tracking margins$99/mo14-day trial
Contractor ForemanSmall crews, budget-conscious owners$49/mo30-day trial

1. Houzz Pro — Best for residential painting contractors

Houzz Pro is a strong fit for painting contractors serving homeowners. Beyond project management, you get direct exposure to homeowners actively looking for painters on the Houzz platform — an integrated marketing channel most competitors don't offer.

Key features for painters

  • Lead generation from Houzz's homeowner marketplace
  • Professional estimates with room-by-room line items
  • Before/after photo documentation in the client portal
  • Online payment and invoice collection
  • Client approval workflows for color selections and change orders

Limitations

  • Not built for commercial painting operations
  • Less robust crew scheduling than field service tools
  • Lead volume depends heavily on your market

Houzz Pro is our top pick for residential painters who want marketing + project management in one platform.

Try Houzz Pro Free

2. Jobber — Best for high-volume painting service companies

If your painting company does dozens of jobs a month — repaints, touch-ups, property management work — Jobber's field service management features are best in class. Scheduling, dispatch, automated client reminders, and online booking all work together smoothly.

Key features for painters

  • Drag-and-drop crew scheduling and dispatch
  • Automated quote follow-up emails that recover leads
  • GPS tracking for crew accountability
  • Client self-booking and online payment
  • Job forms for surface prep checklists and condition documentation

Limitations

  • Weaker job costing than JobTread
  • Less suited for complex multi-phase painting projects

3. Buildertrend — Best for painting subs on construction projects

If you're a painting subcontractor working for general contractors and homebuilders, Buildertrend is the platform your GCs use. Being fluent in it makes you a more attractive sub — you can receive scheduling updates, submit daily logs, and communicate through the platform your clients already live in.

Painting subs who want to win more GC business: Buildertrend integration is a real differentiator.

Try Buildertrend

4. JobTread — Best for margin-focused painting companies

JobTread's real-time job costing helps painting contractors understand exactly what they made on each job. When material prices fluctuate and labor is variable, that visibility is the difference between a profitable year and a lean one.

5. Contractor Foreman — Best budget option

At $49/month, Contractor Foreman gives small painting crews estimates, invoices, time tracking, and project tracking without breaking the bank. It's not as polished as the top picks but it's entirely functional for a 2–5 person painting operation.

Features painting contractors should prioritize

  • Fast estimating: Painting jobs get won or lost on turnaround time — you need estimates in minutes, not hours
  • Crew scheduling: Idle crew is expensive; you need scheduling that fills every day
  • Photo documentation: Before/after photos protect you from "that's not what I wanted" disputes
  • Invoice collection: Online payment reduces collections from weeks to days
  • Material tracking: Paint waste and overages kill margins — track actuals vs. estimates

Bottom line

Residential painters: start with Houzz Pro for the built-in leads, or Jobber if you're doing high volume. Commercial painters and subs: look at JobTread or Buildertrend depending on your scale.
